Waterboy's heart pounded like a drum in his chest as he stumbled into the janitor's closet, the door clicking shut behind him with a soft thud; at least, as soft as he could close it given his... predicament. He stumbled over the mops and buckets, almost losing his footing a couple of times. His mind was still trapped back in the shower-room.

He'd only meant to restock the towels—honest work, nothing more... that's what he was hired to do. But then he'd heard the water running, and curiosity had tugged at him. P
